text:
Problem Overview

In this problem, students are presented with survey information that needs to be written in percent form. They use the percent to predict portions of 500 students that would have the same survey results. Students use strategies to find percentages. The strategies may include ones discussed in previous problems, such as scaling ratios, rate tables, bar diagrams, grids, and double number lines. Students also examine student work to see the advantages of finding 1% as a way to calculate any percent.

Arc of Learning: Exploration, Analysis

Standards: See your state alignment chart.

NWDYK

How does finding 1% help you scale to another percent? How is using a percent bar like using a rate table?

Emerging Mathematical Ideas

  • Continue building expertise in applying a variety of strategies to solving percent problems of increasing complexity.
  • Apply percent representations snd strategies to new contexts with opportunities to
    • make sense of and use information given in the form of a tape diagram;
    • connect the use of unit rates from rate tables with percent calculations scaling up from 1%; and
    • use multiple strategies to solve percent problems.

Launch (Getting Started)

Connecting to Prior Knowledge

Introduce the context of traits in a way that piques students' curiosity. It is important not to focus too much on any individual student but rather to express wonder about the variation in eye color, hair color, earlobes, hairlines, etc. The focus is not on genetics but on the frequency of specific traits in the population.

Presenting the Challenge

The Initial Challenge introduces the context and the math with data on genetic traits geathered from a middle school classroom. If you have the time and inclination, students may be more interested in a data set you collect in your class. This may result in numbers that are harder to work with than those shown in in the Student Edition.

Distribute Learning Aid 4.3: Genetic Traits, and have students work in groups of 3 on this problem.
